rationale: Two separate Living-off-the-Land binaries (certutil and bitsadmin) were used sequentially to download the same file from a raw GitHub URL into the user's Temp directory, both running at High integrity as corp\Administrator (EV-001, EV-002, A0). The redundant download attempts — first via certutil (EV-001/EV-002), then via bitsadmin (A0) — are a classic LOLBin download-cradle pattern used to bypass controls that block one method. The parent chain (PowerShell → cmd → bitsadmin, EV-003/A0) further indicates scripted orchestration. The target file is named as a LICENSE.txt, which could be benign tooling or a renamed payload; the actual file content is not present in the evidence, capping confidence that this is fully malicious rather than a red-team or admin script. The activity warrants immediate investigation to determine the file's true content and the PowerShell session's origin and full command history.
